title: 原來英國國會就香港有人因’雨傘運動‘入冊開咗緊急會議

replies.msg:
.....
The Government was asked an urgent question by Alistair Carmichael, as the nine activists await sentencing. They could face up to seven years in jail for their parts in the peaceful protests, which followed the 2014 announcement by China that it would allow direct elections in 2017, but only from a list of candidates pre-approved by Beijing.

A former British colony, control of Hong Kong was returned to China in 1997 on the proviso that it would retain "a high degree of autonomy, except in foreign and defence affairs". The level of autonomy allowed in practice remains a point of contention between some in Hong Kong and those on the mainland.

Watch Parliament TV: urgent question on pro-democracy activists in Hong Kong
https://parliamentlive.tv/event/index/a3c82a52-dd6b-4396-8fdd-46ce360a2915

Speaking for the Government, Mark Field, the Minister of State said;

“The UK Government is acutely aware of its and our enduring responsibilities to Hong Kong […] we remain absolutely committed to monitoring and ensuring the faithful implementation of [the] joint declaration, and indeed the principle of one country, two systems”

He echoes the concerns raised by the Foreign Secretary that the degree of apolitical autonomy in Hong Kong is being reduced and said, "It would be deeply concerning if this ruling discourages legitimate protest in the future’."

Alistair Carmichael thanked the Minister for his statement, and encouraged an equally robust tone in discussion on the subject with China. He said;

“This prosecution and now conviction of nine leaders of the Umbrella Movement is just the latest in a series of egregious human rights abuses by the Government in China.”

https://www.parliament.uk/business/news/2019/april/government-questioned-after-guilty-verdict-for-pro-democracy-hong-kong-activists/

會議transcripts:
https://hansard.parliament.uk/Commons/2019-04-10/debates/5F3DD016-BD22-4EA6-BB32-9D0C805DB7EA/HongKongPro-DemocracyActivists

https://hansard.parliament.uk/Lords/2019-04-10/debates/C9FAEB26-F73F-428B-80FA-0F7FF7D50AA6/HongKongPro-DemocracyActivists

世柒：

⋯⋯⋯唔係話英國BREXIT好唔得閒，國會三份報告，一個長達四十分鐘嘅緊急辯論，香港立法會係想辯論都辯論唔到，唔好話Urgently Debate。而呢個辯論，尾段係講到 BNO 同界限街問題，呢個證明英國朝野已經思考另一啲做法。

佔中九子 已經觸發英國朝野做嘢

界限街問題，實際係喺游蕙禎MP去信英國國會果陣已經進入視野，但正式擺上大檯討論，居然係工黨議員，完全估佢唔到，尾段咁問：

Indeed the Sino-British joint declaration itself was a great achievement of co-operation and it was done with great sacrifice from the British side, as Hong Kong Island and Kowloon, had been part of the British territories in perpetuity. It was done for practical and diplomatic reasons. Can the Minister reassert the continuing mandate that Britain has to ensure that the Sino-British declaration is respected until July 2047? What practical steps will he take to achieve that?

當然，Mark Field 答係佢會訪問大灣區係有啲答非所問，但證明英國國會議員諗到香港島同九龍實為英國擁有主權嘅問題，因為《南京條約》同《北京條約》只不過被《中英聯合聲明》以Parliament Act(亦即Hong Kong Act 1985)遮蔽，如果Hong Kong Act 1985失效，係會觸發香港島同界限街以南地區回歸英國，甚至西九龍中國佔據地區(高鐵西九站)嘅問題。大家可以諗到，英國國會議員諗嘅方向，唔係純粹我寫「膠觀點」咁簡單

但Mark Field之後主動提出BNO至耐人尋味，佢回應係前駐港英軍嘅子女，喺香港讀書嘅David Morris MP嘅提問，佢問外交部有無辦法阻止引渡

On the rights of British national (overseas) passport holders, my hon. Friend will be aware that the right of abode in the UK is defined under the Immigration Act 1971 and only British citizens and certain British subjects have that right. However, we have ongoing responsibilities to Hong Kong citizens, and even to those who do not enjoy that right of abode, and we will continue to make the strongest of cases to ensure that, up to 2047 and potentially beyond, such rights are properly upheld.

David Morris MP 無問BNO，佢只係以一個香港長大嘅人身份去問(As someone who grew up in Hong Kong)，但 Mark Field 嘅回應主動提到BNO係十分之耐人尋味，佢提到幾個element

Immigration Act 1971 無俾 RoA BNO
BNO 無法用 RoA 走嚟英國
有無其他方法保障 BNO 離開
大家都知，BNO無法享受英國領事保護，所以唯一阻止中國引渡嘅方法，就俾BNO離開，Mark Field暗示佢諗緊唔改變 IA 1971嘅前提下，俾BNO可以前往英國或離開，簡單嚟講，英國已經諗到係救人為先。游說係要，但游說失敗都要諗救人。Mark Field我諗到佢係有幾個方案，簡單嚟講， 佔中九子 加逃犯條例，實際已經trigger咗嘢，所以香港人係必須持續向英國講，唔好只靠泛民，包括做好選民登記，以及寫信俾MP，甚至喺UK in Hong Kong 留comments。要繼續「告洋狀」，用「告洋狀」嚟blackmouth人嘅，根本就係中共嘅人，如果係持英國國籍，遲早要用high treason嚟郁呢啲人。

但可以肯定係，攤牌時刻迫近，否則唔會係咁，香港人唔可以功虧一簣，更加唔好聽某啲仆街老點。

https://martinoei.com/article/26124/%e4%bd%94%e4%b8%ad%e4%b9%9d%e5%ad%90-%e9%80%83%e7%8a%af%e6%a2%9d%e4%be%8b-bno

Indeed the Sino-British joint declaration itself was a great achievement of co-operation and it was done with great sacrifice from the British side, as Hong Kong Island and Kowloon, had been part of the British territories in perpetuity. It was done for practical and diplomatic reasons. Can the Minister reassert the continuing mandate that Britain has to ensure that the Sino-British declaration is respected until July 2047? What practical steps will he take to achieve that?

當然，Mark Field 答係佢會訪問大灣區係有啲答非所問，但證明英國國會議員諗到香港島同九龍實為英國擁有主權嘅問題，因為《南京條約》同《北京條約》只不過被《中英聯合聲明》以Parliament Act(亦即Hong Kong Act 1985)遮蔽，如果Hong Kong Act 1985失效，係會觸發香港島同界限街以南地區回歸英國，甚至西九龍中國佔據地區(高鐵西九站)嘅問題。大家可以諗到，英國國會議員諗嘅方向，唔係純粹我寫「膠觀點」咁簡單

但Mark Field之後主動提出BNO至耐人尋味，佢回應係前駐港英軍嘅子女，喺香港讀書嘅David Morris MP嘅提問，佢問外交部有無辦法阻止引渡

On the rights of British national (overseas) passport holders, my hon. Friend will be aware that the right of abode in the UK is defined under the Immigration Act 1971 and only British citizens and certain British subjects have that right. However, we have ongoing responsibilities to Hong Kong citizens, and even to those who do not enjoy that right of abode, and we will continue to make the strongest of cases to ensure that, up to 2047 and potentially beyond, such rights are properly upheld.

David Morris MP 無問BNO，佢只係以一個香港長大嘅人身份去問(As someone who grew up in Hong Kong)，但 Mark Field 嘅回應主動提到BNO係十分之耐人尋味，佢提到幾個element

Immigration Act 1971 無俾 RoA BNO
BNO 無法用 RoA 走嚟英國
有無其他方法保障 BNO 離開
大家都知，BNO無法享受英國領事保護，所以唯一阻止中國引渡嘅方法，就俾BNO離開，Mark Field暗示佢諗緊唔改變 IA 1971嘅前提下，俾BNO可以前往英國或離開，簡單嚟講，英國已經諗到係救人為先。游說係要，但游說失敗都要諗救人。Mark Field我諗到佢係有幾個方案，簡單嚟講， 佔中九子 加逃犯條例，實際已經trigger咗嘢，所以香港人係必須持續向英國講，唔好只靠泛民，包括做好選民登記，以及寫信俾MP，甚至喺UK in Hong Kong 留comments。要繼續「告洋狀」，用「告洋狀」嚟blackmouth人嘅，根本就係中共嘅人，如果係持英國國籍，遲早要用high treason嚟郁呢啲人。

但可以肯定係，攤牌時刻迫近，否則唔會係咁，香港人唔可以功虧一簣，更加唔好聽某啲仆街老點。

Indeed the Sino-British joint declaration itself was a great achievement of co-operation and it was done with great sacrifice from the British side, as Hong Kong Island and Kowloon, had been part of the British territories in perpetuity. It was done for practical and diplomatic reasons. Can the Minister reassert the continuing mandate that Britain has to ensure that the Sino-British declaration is respected until July 2047? What practical steps will he take to achieve that?
